skill.name: git-guardrails-claude-code
skill.description: Set up Claude Code hooks that block destructive git commands such as push, reset --hard, clean, branch deletion, checkout ., and restore . before they execute.
skill.summary: Install Claude Code hooks that block destructive git commands before they run.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell usage and hidden Claude settings are confirmed as operational risks, not malware. The script reads Claude Code hook input, extracts a command with jq, and blocks selected git patterns; no network access, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was found. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives because SKILL.md line 3 is descriptive text about git reset --hard, not cryptographic code.

medium_findings.title: Claude Code Bash Hook Installation
medium_findings.description: The skill installs a PreToolUse Bash hook in project or global Claude settings. This is the intended function, but a global hook changes behavior across projects and executes the bundled script before Bash tool use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The settings paths and hook commands are explicit in SKILL.md. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it creates persistent command-hook execution.

medium_findings.title: Shell Script Processes Tool Commands
medium_findings.description: The hook script reads JSON from stdin, extracts tool_input.command with jq, and checks the command against regular expressions. It does not execute the extracted command, but it relies on shell utilities during every hook run.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell interpreter, command substitution, jq extraction, and grep check are directly visible. Risk is bounded because the captured command is inspected, not evaluated.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Is False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3 as weak cryptography, but the line only describes blocking git reset --hard. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the referenced line confirms it is skill metadata text. There is no cryptographic API, command, or algorithm at this location.

low_findings.title: Inline Git Command Examples Are Benign Documentation
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts in SKILL.md are inline examples of blocked git commands or setup instructions. They document the guardrail policy rather than executing commands from the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown text and fenced examples. They are relevant commands, but they are not hidden execution paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Claude Settings Modification
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup instructions add a command hook to .claude/settings.json or ~/.claude/settings.json. Users should prefer project scope unless they intentionally want global Bash hook behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly names project and global Claude settings locations. This is expected behavior, but persistent hook configuration is a meaningful marketplace risk factor.

dangerous_patterns.title: Regex-Based Blocking May Be Incomplete
dangerous_patterns.description: The script blocks a fixed list of dangerous git patterns. This reduces risk but is not a complete git policy engine and may miss command variants outside the configured patterns.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The fixed pattern list and grep check are visible in the script. Incompleteness is an operational limitation, not evidence of malicious intent.

content.user_title: Install Git Guardrails for Claude Code
content.value_statement: Destructive git commands can lose work or publish changes too early. This skill installs Claude Code hooks that block risky git operations before execution.
actual_capabilities: Installs a Claude Code PreToolUse hook for Bash commands.
Blocks git push, reset --hard, clean, branch -D, checkout ., and restore . patterns.
Supports project-level and global Claude settings locations.
Provides a bundled Bash script for command inspection.
Includes a verification command for testing the hook behavior.
limitations: Requires Claude Code hook support and a working Bash environment.
Requires jq because the script parses hook input as JSON.
Uses fixed regex patterns, so custom git policies need script edits.
Does not replace repository permissions, protected branches, or code review.

use_cases.title: Protect solo projects from accidental pushes
use_cases.description: Add a local guardrail so Claude Code cannot push or reset work without explicit user action.

use_cases.title: Standardize safer AI coding sessions
use_cases.description: Install a consistent Claude Code hook for teams that want destructive git operations blocked during assisted coding.

use_cases.title: Harden experimental repositories
use_cases.description: Block risky git commands while testing agents, prototypes, or unfamiliar automation in a repository.

prompt_templates.title: Install project guardrails
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up git guardrails for this project only. Ask before changing Claude settings, then verify the hook blocks git push.

prompt_templates.title: Review before global setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the tradeoffs between project and global installation, then install the hook globally only after I confirm the scope.

prompt_templates.title: Customize blocked commands
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the git guardrail hook and add patterns for any extra destructive git commands I list. Keep the existing blocked patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Audit existing hooks
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect my Claude Code hook settings for this repository, merge the git guardrail hook without removing existing hooks, and test the result.

output_examples.input: Install git guardrails for this repository.
output_examples.output: The project hook is installed, executable, and registered in Claude settings. A test git push command is blocked with a clear warning.

output_examples.input: Add reset and clean protection to my Claude Code workflow.
output_examples.output: The hook blocks reset --hard and clean commands before execution. Existing Claude Code settings remain merged with the new hook.

output_examples.input: Check whether my global hook is active.
output_examples.output: The global settings path references the guardrail script, and the verification step confirms that dangerous git commands exit as blocked.

best_practices: Prefer project installation unless every repository needs the same guardrail.
Review existing Claude hooks before merging new hook settings.
Run the verification step after changing blocked command patterns.
anti_patterns: Do not overwrite an existing Claude settings file without preserving other hooks.
Do not treat this hook as a substitute for protected branches or repository permissions.
Do not install globally without understanding the effect on all Claude Code projects.

faq.question: What does this skill block?
faq.answer: It blocks selected destructive git commands, including push, reset --hard, clean, branch -D, checkout ., and restore . patterns.

faq.question: Does the script run the git command?
faq.answer: No. The script reads the requested Bash command, checks it against patterns, and exits before dangerous commands run.

faq.question: Can I install it for one project only?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports project installation through .claude/settings.json and a project hook script path.

faq.question: Can I install it globally?
faq.answer: Yes. Global installation uses ~/.claude/settings.json, but it affects all Claude Code projects for that user.

faq.question: Can I customize the blocked list?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs Claude to ask about adding or removing patterns before editing the copied script.

faq.question: Does it protect against every risky git operation?
faq.answer: No. It blocks configured regex patterns and should be combined with repository permissions and normal review practices.
